problem_statement: Agentic coding produces larger volumes of code per pull request, shifting the burden to human reviewers who must examine AI-generated methods—many of which are eventually deleted during review—thereby increasing reviewer cognitive load.
magnitude_evidence: Of 12,343 methods added at PR creation, 9.9% of methods are deleted in PRs that require revisions (6.0% at method level, 3.9% at file level); Mozannar et al. reported 'verifying suggestions' accounts for 22.4% of total session time, and Becker et al. found AI tool use increased development time by 19%
insufficiency_evidence: GPT-4o's Recall was extremely low (2.6%), misclassifying deleted code as 'Survived' by conflating local code quality with system-level necessity; also Becker et al. found AI use increased development time by 19% due to review/correction
solution_mechanisms: Random Forest classifier predicting methods likely to be deleted during review
23 code-metric features (size, method type, contents) extracted at PR creation
prioritizing reviewer attention on essential code
AST + refactoring detection (ActRef) to label survived vs deleted methods
key_constructs: conflating local code quality with system-level necessity
